paper_title: Adsorption Kinetic and Isotherm Studies of Heavy Metal Ions on Pollen@FePO4

methods.description: This method investigates the kinetics of the adsorption process to provide important information for designing batch adsorption systems, such as the adsorption rate, the performance of the adsorbent, and the rate-limiting step in the sorption process. The study uses three kinetic models: pseudo-first-order, pseudo-second-order, and intraparticle diffusion models.

steps.description: The pseudo-first-order model is generally described by equation (3), which can be transformed to the form as given in equation (4). The straight-line plots of 1/q_t vs 1/t of equation (4) are used to determine the values of k_1, R^2, and q_e,calc.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform linear regression analysis using pseudo-first-order model

steps.description: The pseudo-second-order model is expressed by equation (5). Its linearized form may be represented by equation (6). The plot of t/q_t vs t of equation (6) should give a linear relationship, from which the values of k_2, R^2, and q_e,calc can be determined.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform linear regression analysis using pseudo-second-order model

steps.description: The intraparticle diffusion model describes the intraparticle diffusion process, as presented in equation (7). The plot of q_t vs t^0.5 of equation (7) is used to determine the values of k_id and R^2.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform linear regression analysis using intraparticle diffusion model

methods.description: This method studies the interaction between the adsorbate and the adsorbent to understand the adsorption mechanism. The equilibrium data are analyzed using Langmuir and Freundlich isotherm models.

steps.description: Experiments were carried out by mixing 50 mL of metal solution of varying concentrations with adsorbent at pH 5.92 at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare metal solution and mix with adsorbent

steps.description: The Langmuir isotherm model can be expressed in the linearized form. The values of q_m and b can be calculated from the slope and intercept of the plot of C_e/q_e vs C_e.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Langmuir isotherm constants from linear plot

steps.description: The Freundlich isotherm model can be expressed by an empirical equation. The plot of ln q_e vs ln C_e should be a straight line with a slope of 1/n and an intercept of ln k_F.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Freundlich isotherm constants from linear plot
